summary: Read-only red-team findings for final reporting: high EDD diagnostic rows remain source-exposed, some high-score answers still needed deterministic/manual caveats, and no-judge or one-case probes must not be compared with judged 12-case rows.

models.review_method: Read-only artifact inspection with rg, Get-Content, and limited local JSON parsing attempts; no generator, judge, retriever, evaluator, API, or model call was run.

budget_summary.known_caveat: L84 monolithic timeout may have made calls before writing cost_summary.json, so local observed spend can undercount true spend.

outputs.description: Worker C report caveat and high-score-answer red-team contract.
Compact human-readable summary of the accepted findings.

proposal.bottom_line: The final report should lead with L37 as the only strict scoreboard row and present L95 only as the strongest source-exposed diagnostic candidate. The report should include explicit caveats for qv7_006 and qv7_009 because both show that high groundedness/relevance and high EDD can miss answer-usefulness or safety-tail problems.

accepted_fields_or_changes.title: Source-exposed high scores are diagnostic, not held-out validation
accepted_fields_or_changes.quote_or_short_paraphrase: Smoke preserves strict scoreboard L37 EDD 86.25; L95 is best source-exposed v7 diagnostic with EDD 98.54 and no answer_quality_issues, but is not fresh validation.
accepted_fields_or_changes.report_wording: Report L95 as the strongest source-exposed diagnostic candidate, not as held-out validation or a production champion. The only strict scoreboard score remains L37 EDD 86.25 unless a genuinely fresh cohort is run.

accepted_fields_or_changes.title: qv7_006: correct abstention still had a forbidden-detail tail
accepted_fields_or_changes.quote_or_short_paraphrase: L81 scored EDD 97.41 with coverage/MRR 1.0 and judge 5/5, but qv7_006 refused final vendor/amount and then continued with a claim-flow example and official contact details.
accepted_fields_or_changes.report_wording: Use qv7_006 as the primary example that binary abstention and judge 5/5 can hide unsafe or over-helpful refusal tails. The fix should be described as a deterministic sensitive-tail guard plus no-judge targeted repair, not as a new comparable EDD gain.

accepted_fields_or_changes.title: L83 and L94 EDD values are non-comparable because judge scores were omitted or structurally absent
accepted_fields_or_changes.quote_or_short_paraphrase: L83 EDD 57.16 was intentionally no-judge; L94 is latency/cost evidence only because abstention rows do not receive judge scores.
accepted_fields_or_changes.report_wording: Do not rank L83 or L94 by EDD against judged 12-case rows. Their useful facts are targeted behavior, cost, and latency observations; their EDD scores are smoke/diagnostic artifacts.

accepted_fields_or_changes.title: Judge-blindness: qv6_007 passed ordinary judge while failing claim preservation
accepted_fields_or_changes.quote_or_short_paraphrase: L44 judge gave 5/5, but the claim gate still failed physical_access_control_system as source_supported_but_answer_underanswered; preservation rate 0.5.
accepted_fields_or_changes.report_wording: State that groundedness/relevance judges are necessary but not sufficient. Paired claim-preservation and source-scoped checks are needed because a human-important claim can be missed even under a 5/5 judge.

accepted_fields_or_changes.title: qv7_009: grounded answer was too structured for plain-language intent
accepted_fields_or_changes.quote_or_short_paraphrase: qv7_009 was flagged as plain_language_answer_over_structured despite judge 5/5; L81 baseline had about 1142 chars and 20 list/bullet lines. L89/L91 reduced it to concise plain-language form.
accepted_fields_or_changes.report_wording: Use qv7_009 as the answer-format example: a technically grounded RFP-style checklist can be the wrong answer shape when the user asks for an easy/plain-language explanation. Keep format-fit gates separate from factual judging.

accepted_fields_or_changes.title: Tail latency and timeout risk can be hidden by average EDD
accepted_fields_or_changes.quote_or_short_paraphrase: L84 timed out before artifacts; L85 topk12 had max latency 36.95s and lower judge metrics; L91 still had a qv7_006 28.81s tail despite clean answer issues.
accepted_fields_or_changes.report_wording: Report average latency beside max/tail latency and artifact completeness. A row with good average EDD is not sufficient if the run timed out, cost artifacts are missing, or sensitive cases remain slow.

accepted_fields_or_changes.title: Preemptive sensitive guard is a candidate improvement but carries false-positive risk
accepted_fields_or_changes.quote_or_short_paraphrase: L95 is strongest source-exposed diagnostic row, but next gate is false-positive set or fresh validation; L96 narrowed the preempt marker set to avoid 추정금액 false positives.
accepted_fields_or_changes.report_wording: Describe the preempt guard as promising but not fully validated. Include a preempt false-positive caveat: broad markers can suppress legitimate budget/estimated-amount questions unless guarded by fixtures and fresh tests.

accepted_fields_or_changes.title: Same-issuer and source-scope cases need source-purity caveats even with perfect retrieval metrics
accepted_fields_or_changes.quote_or_short_paraphrase: qv6_001 retrieved only the same org but mixed UICC with another same-issuer project; project-title focus was required. Source markers must be tied to the target row, not the whole CSV.
accepted_fields_or_changes.report_wording: Add a source-purity caveat: org coverage and retrieved-source labels do not guarantee the answer used the right project row. Same-issuer project focus and target-bound source checks should be reported as required safeguards.

recommended_report_section.heading: Caveats and Red-Team Examples
recommended_order: latency tail and timeout/cost artifact risk

commands_run: Get-ChildItem -Force current run dir and worker_outputs
rg -n for qv7_006, qv7_009, L81-L96, judge, latency, source, EDD, false-positive terms
Get-Content prior checkpoint_03_merge_decisions.md, checkpoint_04_smoke.md, summary.md, l47/l48-l76 reports, and L82-L83/L84-L86/L91 reports

Attempted ConvertFrom-Json extracts from selected details.json files; PowerShell reported invalid-array parsing errors on prior artifacts, so compact evidence was taken from report/checkpoint text and targeted rg snippets instead
No model, generator, judge, retriever, evaluator, or API command was run
observed_results: Current worker_outputs initially contained guard_false_positive.contract.json only.
Prior smoke says final aggregate rows=49, scoreboard_rows=1, diagnostic_only_rows=48, smoke pass, and latest no-API fixture pack 28/28.
Prior smoke says L95 EDD 98.54, latency 14.44s, qv7_009 clean, qv7_006/qv7_012 preempted, and all answer_quality_issues empty, while explicitly not fresh validation.
L82-L83 report says qv7_006 was a high-score L81 issue: refused some forbidden fields but continued with detailed claim-flow/contact tail; L83 no-judge targeted repair cleared answer_quality_issues.
L48-L76 summary says qv7_009 was grounded and judge 5/5 but over-structured for plain-language intent; L89/L91 are candidate repairs, not fresh validation.
L84-L86 report says monolithic L84 timed out before artifacts and topk12 had worse latency tail plus lower groundedness/relevance.
